acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Dot allocation in mutation planning performs no per-dot `meta.json` sync writes
- [ ] Counter durability is persisted atomically inside WAL index commit path
- [ ] Bench/regression test demonstrates reduced fsync frequency on mutation hotpath
description:
**Problem**
`RuntimeDotAllocator::next_dot()` triggers synchronous `meta.json` durable write (temp write + fsync + rename + dir fsync) on each allocation, creating avoidable hotpath I/O and latency amplification.

**Files:** `crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/executor/helpers.rs`, `crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/store/runtime.rs`, `crates/beads-daemon-core/src/wal/index.rs`
design: Move OR-set counter durability into unified WAL index transaction metadata and keep allocator increments in-memory during planning. Use typed durable mutation commit to include counter advancement atomically with other WAL index state.
title: Remove per-dot meta fsync from runtime allocator hotpath
